title: Abdominal wall surface anatomy
body_html:
Abdominal regions
The abdomen can either be divided into 4 quadrants using the midline and a horizontal line through the umbilicus or a 9 region scheme can be used. The 9 region scheme is favoured by the college and covered in further detail.

Image sourced from Wikipedia
The 9 region abdominal anatomical scheme uses the vertical midclavicular lines and horizontal transpyloric and transtubercular planes to divide the abdomen into 9 regions


Planes of the abdomen

The planes of the abdomen with the transpyloric plane highlighted

Important abdominal planes and their vertebral levels
  • Transplyloric plane: L1
  • Transtubercular plane: L5

Transpyloric plane

Image sourced from Wikipedia

The transpyloric plane is a horizontal plane located halfway between the suprasternal notch of the manubrium and the upper border of the symphysis pubis. The plane can be considered to lie at the following levels/surface markings:
  • L1 vertebraI level
  • 1 hands width beneath the xiphisternum
  • Midway between the xiphisternum and the umbilicus
  • medial ends of 9th costal cartilages

The transpyloric plane passes through the following abdominal structures:

  • L1 vertebrae
  • Fundus of gallbladder
  • Spinal cord termination
  • Neck of pancreas
  • Origin of superior mesenteric artery from the abdominal aorta and termination of the superior mesenteric vein at the hepatic portal vein
  • Colic flexure
  • Kidney hila
  • Root of the transverse mesocolon
  • 1st part of the duodenum
  • Spleen
  • Pylorus of the stomach
  • Cisterna chyli
